records.title: Buying a home: official checklist
records.description: The home-buying process covers borrowing capacity, finding and assessing a property, making an offer, agreeing conditions, signing the purchase contract and completing the transfer at a notary.

official_source.title: Woning kopen: wat moet ik regelen?

records.ai_summary: Start with a realistic budget, investigate the property before bidding and make important conditions explicit in the written purchase agreement. Ownership changes only after the notarial deed is registered.

records.title: Maximum mortgage and borrowing capacity
records.description: The maximum mortgage depends on both household income and the appraised value of the property, while other financial obligations and the applicable annual lending standards also affect capacity.

official_source.title: Hoeveel kan ik maximaal lenen voor mijn koopwoning?

records.ai_summary: Borrowing capacity is not the same as an affordable personal budget. A lender checks income, property value, interest conditions and disclosed debts under current rules before issuing a binding offer.

records.title: Preparing a mortgage application
records.description: A mortgage application requires a suitable property and financial evidence, followed by affordability and property-value checks by the lender before the loan and mortgage deed can be completed.

official_source.title: Hypotheek aanvragen: wat moet ik regelen?

records.ai_summary: Prepare proof of income, debts, savings and the property transaction, then compare the offer and conditions carefully. A financing condition in the purchase contract can protect the buyer if arranged correctly.

records.title: Cooling-off period after buying a home
records.description: A private buyer has a statutory cooling-off period after receiving the signed purchase agreement and can withdraw without giving a reason or paying compensation during that period.

official_source.title: Hoeveel bedenktijd heb ik na het kopen van een woning?

records.ai_summary: Record when the fully signed contract was received because that starts the statutory calculation. The official page explains how weekends and public holidays affect the end of the cooling-off period.

records.title: One-off and recurring home-buying costs
records.description: A buyer should budget beyond the purchase price for possible inspection, valuation, advice, mortgage and notary costs, transfer tax, insurance, municipal taxes, maintenance and any leasehold charge.

official_source.title: Met welke kosten krijg ik te maken als ik een huis koop?

records.ai_summary: Create a budget that separates purchase, financing and ongoing ownership costs. Current tax rates and thresholds should always be checked at the official source immediately before the transaction.

records.title: Transfer tax when buying a home
records.description: Transfer tax is normally handled through the notary when ownership is transferred. The applicable rate or exemption depends on factors such as intended use, age and current value thresholds.

official_source.title: Moet ik overdrachtsbelasting betalen als ik een huis koop?

records.ai_summary: Confirm the current transfer-tax treatment with the Belastingdienst and notary before signing the deed. Eligibility for a reduced rate or exemption must be assessed for each buyer separately.

records.title: Research ownership and property records
records.description: Kadaster information can show registered ownership, rights, restrictions, leasehold and past transaction details that help a prospective buyer investigate a property before committing.
records.ai_summary: Use current Kadaster products to check the registered property and compare relevant sales information. Registry information supports due diligence but does not replace a structural inspection or legal advice.

records.title: Check the home's energy label
records.description: A valid registered energy label describes the home's standardised energy performance and improvement options and must generally be supplied when an existing home is sold or newly rented.
records.ai_summary: Verify the registered label and its validity, but also inspect the actual insulation, installations and expected household use. Standardised label consumption is not the same as the future resident's energy bill.

records.title: Transfer ownership at the notary
records.description: The notary investigates the parties and property, manages the transaction funds, prepares the transfer and mortgage deeds and registers the deeds in the Kadaster public registers.

official_source.title: De overdracht van een woning bij de notaris

records.ai_summary: Before completion, review the settlement statement and draft deeds and perform the agreed final inspection. The buyer becomes registered owner through the signed deed and its registration by the notary.

records.title: Tax treatment of home-purchase costs
records.description: Some financing costs connected with an owner-occupied home may be deductible, while acquisition costs such as the purchase price and transfer tax generally follow different tax treatment.
records.ai_summary: Keep invoices and distinguish acquisition costs from mortgage-financing costs for the tax return. Use the current Belastingdienst guidance for the tax year because eligibility and limits can change.
